products.item_name: No. 15 Vanille Infinie Perfume Oil
products.description:
No. 15 Vanille Infinie Perfume Oil from Maison Louis Marie. Opens with a golden glow of citron and soft jasmine, unfolding into comforting, warm notes of heliotrope, sugared amber, and creamy tonka. At its heart lies golden vanilla, delicate and radiant, anchored by cashmere oud and a whisper of cloud musk. Vegan, cruelty-free, and alcohol-free.

- Top Notes: Citron / evening jasmine bloom

- Middle Notes: Heliotrope petals / tonka bean / sugared amber

- Base Notes: Cashmere oud / vanilla flower / cloud musk

- Weight: 0.5 fl oz

- Fragrance type: for him or her

Direction to use: Use roll-on perfume oil any time you desire. Apply to pulse points (neck, chest, and wrist), lightly rolling the ball on the skin to release the fragrance.

On the outside, a front zip pocket offers quick access, while mesh side pockets with elastic bungee cords keep bottles or gear secure. Padded shoulder straps, a chest strap, and breathable mesh back panel.

- 100% Polyester

- 22 in x 5.1 in x 12.6 in

products.description:

The body is knitted to be thick and stable, providing a firm fit, while the sleeves are crafted with a soft volume to create a three-dimensional line. The deep armholes emphasize the curve from the shoulder to the sleeve, and the slightly raised mock neck design adds a sophisticated touch. The two-way zipper allows for versatile styling, and the wool blend material with its substantial thickness offers excellent warmth, making it a perfect piece to use as outerwear.

- 85% Modacrylic / 15% polyester

- Hillary is 5’8” and wears size 2, shown in size 0 with Amomento Charcoal Two Tuck Balloon Pants and Vintage shoes (available in-store only).

products.item_name: Apartamento Cookbook #9
products.description:
For the ninth edition of our annual Apartamento cookbook, we’re celebrating Sandwiches! Both versatile and scrumptious, these 16 recipes delight with versions hot and cold, open and closed, savoury, veggie, overloaded BBQ, and one classically sweet ice cream treat.

From white bread to the best rye you can find, we are fascinated to learn how chefs spanning a range of countries and cuisines have gone above and beyond to infuse these easy bites with extra deliciousness.

Drawings by Orfeo Tagiuri.

Featuring: Alex Loveless, Alison Roman, Anthony Ha & Sadie Burns, Bre Graham, Canvis Nous, Cynthia Shanmugalingam, Imogen Kwok, Jago Rackham, Jason Stewart, Jiro Hsu & Michael Janczewski, Nadia Gilbert, Naiara Sabandar, Nicolai Nørregaard, Rirkrit Tiravanija, Sumin Kim, and Zélikha Dinga.

We will donate 20% of the profits generated by this book to American Near East Refugee Aid (ANERA), a non-governmental organization that helps refugees and neglected communities in Palestine, Lebanon, and Jordan.

Published by Apartamento Publishing S.L.
First edition: October 24, 2024
Dimensions: 170 x 240mm
Pages: 40 pages
Binding: hard cover + dust jacket
ISBN: 978-84-09-65183-2

products.item_name: Liguria: Recipes & Wanderings Along the Italian Riviera
products.description:
Liguria with its herbs hugging the sea cliffs, with its bays full of fish, with its ancient olive groves, grapevines, and orchards found in every village.

LIGURIA: Recipes & Wanderings Along the Italian Riviera is the first in a new series, developed in partnership with Belmond, offering an exclusive window into culinary communities across the globe. Featuring vivid photography by Laura Jane Coulson alongside essays by Andy Baraghani, Corrado Corti, Gilda Bruno, and Luca Cesari, the book explores key ingredients, people, and histories which make this rugged coastline so irresistible. From the world of Apartamento, an introduction by Ruth Rogers shares impressions gathered over decades of walking Liguria’s seaside paths, and two guest recipes from Fanny Singer and Max Rocha show how admires from afar might incorporate Ligurian cuisine into their kitchens.

LIGURIA presents 24 classic dishes inspired by the kitchens of Belmond’s hotels in Portofino: Splendido, set in the hills overlooking the bay, and Splendido Mare, down in the piazzetta. We invite you into a magical, transformative land; as Gilda writes, ‘In truth, no one knows Portofino like the sea. Its tides and roaring belly were the only witnesses to the village’s metamorphosis into the most evocative manifestation of Italy’s idealised lifestyle, nonchalant elegance, and sun-drenched, soothing leisure: la dolce vita’.

Published by Apartamento Publishing S.L.
First edition: July 4, 2024
Dimensions: 190 x 240 mm
Pages: 176 pages
Binding: hardcover
ISBN: 978-84-09-61968-9

products.item_name: Apollo: State-of-the-art cooking and a party
products.description:
Apollo: State-of-the-art cooking and a party by Frederik Bille Brahe is the first cookbook devoted to Apollo, his museum restaurant located in the courtyard of the historical art institution Kunsthal Charlottenborg in Copenhagen. Together with head chef Yuta Kurahashi, Frederik shares 104 recipes in Apollo’s signature style: playful, elegant, and rooted in a dialogue between Danish cooking and Japanese, French, and Italian cuisines. With its colourful palette and defining sense of chic, green gastronomy, Apollo follows the much-loved and low-key All the Stuff We Cooked and the wildly successful Atelier September: A Place for Daytime Cooking. Perfect for dinner party cooking, this is Frederik at his most expressive and collaborative. Edited by Jeni Porter and captured on film by photographer Nikolaj Møller, Apollo also explores how a group of talented friends came together to create the space, its textures, artworks, and atmosphere—setting the scene for a restaurant where delicious food, friends, music, art, design, and wine all meet, collide, clash, and coalesce. Apollo is that really good party you never want to end.

About Frederik Bille Brahe
Frederik Bille Brahe is a Copenhagen-born fine-dining trained chef who worked in London and Paris before returning home to follow his dream of opening a very special small café where everyone is his guest. He opened Atelier September in Gothersgade in October 2013, expanding it to other locations in the Danish capital and using it as a launching pad for other ventures, including Apollo in the historic art space Kunsthal Charlottenborg and Sofi bakery in Berlin. His first collection of recipes, All the Stuff We Cooked, was an instant success, and his followup cookbook, Atelier September: A place for daytime cooking, blended recipes with an exclusive poetic chronicling of the first decade of Atelier September, as well as a documentation of his philosophy for gastronomy and life in general.

Published by Apartamento Publishing S.L.
Release date: November 27, 2025
Dimensions: 215 x 297mm
Pages: 320
Binding: hardcover
ISBN: 978-84-09-78307-6
